description_long: This is a medieval-style great helm made primarily of steel, featuring a cylindrical shape with a flat top and slightly flared base. The front is adorned with an ornate, gold-colored cross-shaped reinforcement that covers the face and provides a narrow horizontal slit for vision. The surface of the helmet is metallic gray, showing signs of wear and subtle scratches, with rivets lining the top and sides for structural integrity. The sides and back are plain, lacking any additional decoration or openings. The helmet appears solid and robust, designed to fully enclose and protect the wearer's head. There are no visible ventilation holes, retention straps, or modern features.
description: A medieval steel great helm with a gold cross-shaped faceplate and a worn metallic finish, fully enclosing the head. The helmet is cylindrical with rivets and a narrow eye slit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the curved steel surface, rivets along the top, and part of the gold cross detail extending from the front.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the ornate gold cross-shaped faceplate, narrow eye slit, and the helmet's symmetrical cylindrical form.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with visible rivets and the edge of the gold cross, showing the helmet's solid construction.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying a plain, unadorned metallic surface with rivets along the seam and top edge.
attribute_graphic_design: Ornate gold cross motif on the front; otherwise plain metallic finish with no additional graphics.
